identity.short_name: Ideal-lattice orbits under Aut(F2[x,y]/(x,y)^2) -> digit-sum power

subdomain(s): ideal-lattice group actions and orbit counting (Burnside)

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Count Aut(R)-orbits of ideals with dim_F2(I/2I)=2 in Z4[x]/(x^3,2x)); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (GCD  discriminant  digit sum  engineered derivative  splitting field affine-log modulus) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Classify ideals in the small local ring F2[x,y]/(x,y)^2 and count Aut-orbits using Burnside to obtain T, then use T inside a quadratic discriminant and digit-sum power U^T modulo 1000.
givens: R=F_2[x,y]/(x,y)^2; I is the set of all ideals; G=Aut(R).
T is the number of G-orbits on I.
q(x)=x^2-Tx+1; U is digit sum of |Disc(q)|; output U^T mod 1000.

parameter(s).name: ring
parameter(s).purpose: small ideal lattice with GL2(F2) symmetry

parameter(s).name: base_ring
parameter(s).purpose: controls 2-torsion and nilpotence behavior (here forces 2 \cdot =0 for proper ideals).

parameter(s).name: invariance_scope
parameter(s).purpose: shifts from "Aut-invariant ideals to counting orbits under the full automorphism action.

parameter(s).name: mod_p_dimension_constraint
parameter(s).purpose: selects a constrained stratum of ideals (here p=2, dimension 2).

parameter(s).name: orbit_count_method
parameter(s).purpose: specifies orbit enumeration via fixed-point counts.

parameter(s).name: P_coeffs
parameter(s).domain: integer coefficient list defining a degree-6 polynomial in [x]
parameter(s).purpose: determines the gcd F and hence δ and γ

parameter(s).name: Q_coeffs
parameter(s).domain: integer coefficient list defining a degree-6 polynomial in [x]
parameter(s).purpose: determines the gcd F and hence δ and γ

parameter(s).name: base_prime_p
parameter(s).purpose: sets characteristic for the formal derivative and determines finite-field sizes p^d

parameter(s).name: exponent_template
parameter(s).domain: f(x)=x^{pδ}+c x^{γ+1}-x with c=(γ+1)^{-1} in F_p
parameter(s).purpose: forces derivative cancellation of the pδ term and normalizes the x^{γ} coefficient to 1

parameter(s).name: log_base
parameter(s).purpose: fixes the logarithm base so log_{p^2}(p^d)=d/2 and the modulus uses 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: ideals_correspond_to_subspaces
invariant(s).statement: Because m^2=0, ideals between 0 and m correspond to F2-subspaces of m.

invariant(s).name: Aut_is_GL2_F2
invariant(s).statement: Aut(R) acts as GL2(F2) on m, permuting 1D subspaces.

invariant(s).name: quadratic_discriminant_digit_sum
invariant(s).statement: Disc(x^2-Tx+1)=T^2-4; U is digit sum of |T^2-4|.

invariant(s).name: normal_form_in_R
invariant(s).statement: Every element has a unique representative a+bx+cx^2 with aZ/4Z and b,cF2, since x^3=0 and 2x=0.

invariant(s).name: maximal_ideal_and_2_annihilation
invariant(s).statement: The unique maximal ideal is =(2,x) consisting of elements with even constant term, and 2=0. Hence for any proper ideal I⊆, we have 2I=0 so I/2I≅I as F2-vector spaces.

invariant(s).name: ideal_as_T_invariant_subspace
invariant(s).statement: For proper ideals I⊆, the ideal condition is equivalent to I being an F2-subspace of  invariant under multiplication-by-x, i.e. T(I)⊆I where T(y)=xy.

invariant(s).name: automorphisms_determined_by_image_of_x
invariant(s).statement: Any automorphism fixes constants and is determined by xx+x^2+2 with ,F2; thus |Aut(R)|=4. Additionally x^2 is fixed by all automorphisms because (x+x^2+2)^2=x^2.

invariant(s).name: gcd_uniqueness_in_Zx
invariant(s).statement: F(x)=gcd(P,Q) is uniquely determined up to ±1, fixed by requiring positive leading coefficient.

invariant(s).name: discriminant_is_integer
invariant(s).statement: For quadratic F(x)=ax^2+bx+c in [x], =b^2-4ac is an integer determined by coefficients.

invariant(s).name: derivative_cancellation_in_char_p
invariant(s).statement: In characteristic p, (x^{pδ})' = pδ \cdot x^{pδ-1} = 0, so the x^{pδ} term vanishes in f'(x).

invariant(s).name: engineered_inverse_for_unit_leading_term
invariant(s).statement: With c=(γ+1)^{-1} in _p, we have (c x^{γ+1})' = c(γ+1)x^γ = x^γ, forcing f'(x)=x^γ-1.

invariant(s).name: splitting_of_xn_minus_1
invariant(s).statement: If gcd(p,γ)=1, then x^γ-1 splits over _{p^d} iff γ | (p^d-1). The minimal such d is ord_γ(p).

invariant(s).name: log_p2_prime_power
invariant(s).statement: If m=p^d then log_{p^2}(m)=d/2, hence 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d is an integer.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): All ideals are considered as sets; automorphisms act by image of generators.
Automorphisms preserve 1 and the defining relations x^3=0, 2x=0.
The quotient I/2I is viewed as an F2-vector space via RR/2R.
Proper ideals lie in the maximal ideal  and thus satisfy 2I=0 in this ring.
The computed gcd F(x) has degree 2 (quadratic).
γ+1 is nonzero in _p (equivalently p ∤ (γ+1)), so c=(γ+1)^{-1} exists.
gcd(p,γ)=1 so x^γ-1 has distinct roots and the roots-of-unity splitting criterion applies cleanly.
m is taken among finite fields _{p^d} (prime-power sizes).
The bridge quantity T computed from component A is guaranteed to be an integer in the admissible range required by component B (e.g., as an index, bound, or group-action parameter).
uniqueness_certificate(s): Burnside's lemma over the finite group Aut(R) yields a unique orbit count T.
Given T, the discriminant and digit sum are unique, hence U^T mod 1000 is unique.

The constrained set  is finite and is completely determined by the invariant-subspace description: dim_F2(I/2I)=2 ⇔ |I|=4 ⇔ I is a 2D T-invariant subspace of  under T(y)=xy. This yields a finite explicit list.

Aut(R) is finite and explicitly parameterized by (,)F2^2. The orbit count is uniquely determined by the action on the explicit list of ideals, equivalently by Burnsides lemma applied to fixed-point counts.

F is uniquely determined as the gcd in ℤ[x] with positive leading coefficient, hence δ and γ are deterministic. The coefficient c=(γ+1)^{-1} is unique in _p, so f' is uniquely x^γ-1. The smallest extension degree d for splitting is uniquely ord_γ(p), hence m=p^d is unique. Since 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d and γ are fixed, the modulus d+γ and the residue δ mod (d+γ) are uniquely determined.
(N1 necessity) If T is not computed from component A, component B has multiple admissible outputs across different possible parameter choices; thus the composite is ill-posed without the bridge.
(N2 non-factorization) The final answer is not a trivial Combine(ansA, ansB): the computed T restricts/changes the admissible branch structure inside component B before its output is even defined.

technique(s).name: ideal_lattice_under_GL2_action
technique(s).purpose: enumerate ideals and classify action on 1D subspaces

technique(s).name: digit_sum_of_discriminant_then_modular_power
technique(s).purpose: compute U and then U^T mod 1000

technique(s).name: explicit_element_normal_form_and_ideal_size_bounds
technique(s).purpose: obtain element normal forms and deduce finiteness and sizes.

technique(s).name: reduce_dim_constraint_via_2_annihilation
inputs: fact 2=0 and I⊆ for proper ideals
technique(s).purpose: simplify dim_F2(I/2I) into a pure size/dimension condition on I.

technique(s).name: ideals_as_invariant_subspaces_under_multiplication_map
inputs: as an F2-vector space; multiplication-by-x map T
technique(s).purpose: classify ideals by linear algebra (T-invariant subspaces).
outputs: explicit list of all ideals in

technique(s).name: compute_AutR_via_generator_image
technique(s).purpose: enumerate Aut(R) by admissible images of x and invertibility mod ^2.

technique(s).name: burnside_orbit_count
inputs: finite group action Aut(R) on the finite set
technique(s).purpose: compute number of orbits via fixed-point counts.

technique(s).name: compute_gcd_by_structured_difference
technique(s).purpose: compute F=gcd(P,Q) using that gcd divides PQ and visibly divides both polynomials

technique(s).name: digit_sum_evaluation
technique(s).purpose: compute γ as the sum of decimal digits of δ

technique(s).name: formal_derivative_engineering_in_char_p
technique(s).purpose: use characteristic-p cancellation and inverse normalization to force f'(x)=x^γ-1

technique(s).name: minimal_splitting_field_for_xn_minus_1
technique(s).purpose: find minimal d such that γ | (p^d-1), i.e. d=ord_γ(p), so m=p^d

theorem(s).name: GL2(F2)_is_S3
theorem(s).role: cycle-type classification for the action on 1D subspaces

theorem(s).name: Automorphisms of a finitely generated quotient ring are determined by generator images
theorem(s).role: reduces Aut(R) computation to choices of φ(x) satisfying relations and invertibility.

theorem(s).name: Ideal containment in maximal ideal for finite local rings
theorem(s).role: ensures proper ideals lie in , enabling the 2-annihilation simplification.

theorem(s).name: Euclidean_algorithm_in_Zx
theorem(s).role: defines and guarantees uniqueness of gcd up to units in [x]

theorem(s).name: formal_derivative_in_characteristic_p
theorem(s).role: (x^{pk})' = 0 and (x^n)' = n x^{n-1} with coefficients reduced mod p

theorem(s).name: roots_of_unity_splitting_criterion
theorem(s).role: x^n-1 splits over _q iff n | (q-1) when gcd(n,char)=1

theorem(s).name: multiplicative_order_mod_n
theorem(s).role: minimal d with p^d  1 (mod n) equals ord_n(p)

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Ideal closure under multiplication (encoded as invariance under multiplication-by-x).
Finite group actions and symmetry (orbit counting via Burnside).

critical_trick(s): Model Aut(R) as GL2(F2) acting on the three 1D ideals and use Burnside by cycle type.
Bridge the orbit count T into both the coefficient and exponent in the digit-sum expression.
Finish with a small modular exponentiation.
Bridge Lemma (explicit): compute T in component A, then substitute this specific T into component B; justify N1 and N2 to rule out stapling.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: basic modular arithmetic and combinatorial counts

library(s).name: sage
library(s).reason: optional verification via quotient rings, ideal enumeration, and automorphism group computation.

import_hint(s): # optional: from sageall import Integers, PolynomialRing
from sympy import symbols, Poly, gcd, discriminant, GF

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Execute the intended structural route in a CAS-friendly way: compute bridge T from component A using symbolic identities or explicit enumeration, then compute component B output with the substituted T, finally reduce mod 1000; confirm matches the recorded Answer.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independent cross-check: avoid reusing the same derivation as primary. Use a different route (e.g., brute-force enumeration over the finite search space, numeric root approximation with direct evaluation, or an alternate invariant/Burnside count) to recompute the final answer and compare.
